domain: Hepatocellular Carcinoma Formation Mechanisms and Liver Cancer Cell Variation Mechanisms

subspaces.name: Hepatocyte Dedifferentiation and Transdifferentiation Pathways
subspaces.description: Mechanisms by which mature hepatocytes lose identity or convert into progenitor-like or biliary-like states during HCC initiation, driven by transcriptional reprogramming (e.g., NOTCH, TGFβ, YAP) and epigenetic remodeling.
seed_papers: Zhang et al., Cell Stem Cell 2021;30(2):226–241
Sekiya & Suzuki, Nat Rev Gastroenterol Hepatol 2022;19(5):297–312
subspaces.probe_query: hepatocyte plasticity NOTCH signaling HCC TGFβ-induced EMT YAP/TAZ hepatocyte reprogramming biliary transdifferentiation SOX9+ progenitor cells

subspaces.name: Chronic Liver Injury Microenvironment Drivers
subspaces.description: Role of persistent inflammation, fibrosis, oxidative stress, and dysbiotic microbiota in creating a pro-tumorigenic niche that selects for mutant hepatocytes and fuels clonal expansion.
seed_papers: Llovet et al., Nat Rev Clin Oncol 2021;18(9):569–587
subspaces.probe_query: liver fibrosis HCC senescent cell secretome IL-6/STAT3 inflammation gut-liver axis HCC macrophage polarization ROS-induced mutagenesis

subspaces.name: Clonal Evolution and Intratumoral Heterogeneity
subspaces.description: Genomic, transcriptomic, and phenotypic diversification within HCC tumors over time and space—driven by branching evolution, subclonal driver events, and selective pressures—including spatially resolved mapping of functional variation.
subspaces.probe_query: multi-region sequencing HCC subclonal CTNNB1 TP53 subclones spatial transcriptomics liver cancer phylogenetic reconstruction HCC cancer stem cell hierarchy

subspaces.name: Metabolic Reprogramming in Preneoplastic Hepatocytes
subspaces.description: Early metabolic shifts—including mitochondrial dysfunction, altered lipid handling, glutamine dependency, and glycolytic flux—in hepatocytes under metabolic stress (e.g., NAFLD/NASH) that promote genomic instability and survival advantage.
seed_papers: Pikarsky et al., Nat Rev Cancer 2022;22(10):567–582
subspaces.probe_query: NAFLD-HCC transition mitochondrial ROS HCC ACLY/ACC inhibition lipotoxicity DNA damage glutaminase GLS1 PPARα dysfunction

subspaces.name: Epigenetic Drivers of Hepatocarcinogenesis
subspaces.description: Heritable non-genetic alterations—including aberrant DNA methylation (e.g., global hypomethylation, CpG island hypermethylation), histone modifications (EZH2, KDM6A), and chromatin accessibility changes—that initiate or maintain malignant transformation independent of mutations.
seed_papers: Villanueva et al., Nat Rev Gastroenterol Hepatol 2019;16(1):37–54
subspaces.probe_query: TET2 loss HCC EZH2 oncogenic role DNMT3A mutations HCC H3K27me3 redistribution enhancer hijacking HCC CpG island methylator phenotype

subspaces.name: Oncogenic Signaling Convergence Nodes
subspaces.description: Core signaling modules—especially WNT/β-catenin, RAS/MAPK, PI3K/AKT/mTOR, and TERT regulatory networks—whose co-activation, feedback loops, or compensatory rewiring drive sustained proliferation and evasion of senescence in pre-malignant and malignant hepatocytes.
seed_papers: Schmidt et al., Nat Rev Cancer 2021;21(12):751–770
subspaces.probe_query: CTNNB1 mutation HCC TERT promoter mutation AXIN1 loss RAS pathway HCC PI3KCA amplification mTORC1 hyperactivation

subspaces.name: Liver Cancer Stem Cell Niche Regulation
subspaces.description: Specialized microanatomical compartments—such as perivascular, periportal, or fibrotic border zones—that sustain liver cancer stem cells via Notch, Hedgehog, hypoxia (HIF2α), and integrin-mediated adhesion signals, enabling therapy resistance and recurrence.
seed_papers: Haraguchi et al., Cell Stem Cell 2013;13(1):101–113
subspaces.probe_query: CD133+ HCC cells EpCAM+ tumor-initiating cells HIF2α-VEGF niche integrin α6β1 Notch-JAG1 signaling peri-vascular niche

subspaces.name: Viral Integration and Oncogenic Viral Mechanisms
subspaces.description: Mechanistic consequences of HBV DNA integration—including structural variants, enhancer hijacking (e.g., TERT, MLL4), HBx-mediated disruption of p53/DNA repair, and chronic immune-mediated injury—that directly contribute to hepatocyte transformation in endemic regions.
subspaces.probe_query: HBV integration sites HBx-p53 interaction TERT promoter integration MLL4 rearrangement viral enhancer insertion HBV cccDNA persistence

user_interaction.question: Select the subspaces to prioritize before ZhiZhi imports papers. You can also add custom subspaces.

options.label: Hepatocyte Dedifferentiation and Transdifferentiation Pathways
options.description: Mechanisms by which mature hepatocytes lose identity or convert into progenitor-like or biliary-like states during HCC initiation, driven by transcriptional reprogramming (e.g., NOTCH, TGFβ, YAP) and epigenetic remodeling.

options.label: Chronic Liver Injury Microenvironment Drivers
options.description: Role of persistent inflammation, fibrosis, oxidative stress, and dysbiotic microbiota in creating a pro-tumorigenic niche that selects for mutant hepatocytes and fuels clonal expansion.

options.label: Clonal Evolution and Intratumoral Heterogeneity
options.description: Genomic, transcriptomic, and phenotypic diversification within HCC tumors over time and space—driven by branching evolution, subclonal driver events, and selective pressures—including spatially resolved mapping of functional variation.

options.label: Metabolic Reprogramming in Preneoplastic Hepatocytes
options.description: Early metabolic shifts—including mitochondrial dysfunction, altered lipid handling, glutamine dependency, and glycolytic flux—in hepatocytes under metabolic stress (e.g., NAFLD/NASH) that promote genomic instability and survival advantage.

options.label: Epigenetic Drivers of Hepatocarcinogenesis
options.description: Heritable non-genetic alterations—including aberrant DNA methylation (e.g., global hypomethylation, CpG island hypermethylation), histone modifications (EZH2, KDM6A), and chromatin accessibility changes—that initiate or maintain malignant transformation independent of mutations.

options.label: Oncogenic Signaling Convergence Nodes
options.description: Core signaling modules—especially WNT/β-catenin, RAS/MAPK, PI3K/AKT/mTOR, and TERT regulatory networks—whose co-activation, feedback loops, or compensatory rewiring drive sustained proliferation and evasion of senescence in pre-malignant and malignant hepatocytes.

options.label: Liver Cancer Stem Cell Niche Regulation
options.description: Specialized microanatomical compartments—such as perivascular, periportal, or fibrotic border zones—that sustain liver cancer stem cells via Notch, Hedgehog, hypoxia (HIF2α), and integrin-mediated adhesion signals, enabling therapy resistance and recurrence.

options.label: Viral Integration and Oncogenic Viral Mechanisms
options.description: Mechanistic consequences of HBV DNA integration—including structural variants, enhancer hijacking (e.g., TERT, MLL4), HBx-mediated disruption of p53/DNA repair, and chronic immune-mediated injury—that directly contribute to hepatocyte transformation in endemic regions.

custom_subspace_input.placeholder: e.g. Demand Response; EV Charging Coordination; Building Energy Management
custom_subspace_input.instructions: If your target subfield is not listed, provide one subspace per line or semicolon-separated. These will be converted into custom retrieval branches.

user_interaction.continue_with: Pass subspace_map_id plus selected_subfields, or pass option labels as focus_branches to run_zhizhi_literature_analysis.
